title: New large value estimates for Dirichlet polynomials

thm:1.1.summary: Large values estimate: if $|b_n|\le1$ and $(t_r)_{r\le R}$ are $1$-separated in $[0,T]$ with $|\sum_{n=N}^{2N}b_n n^{it_r}|\ge V$ for all $r$, then $R\le T^{o(1)}(N^2V^{-2}+N^{18/5}V^{-4}+TN^{12/5}V^{-4})$. The headline bound; new in the critical range $V\approx N^{3/4}$.

thm:1.2.summary: Zero-density estimate $N(\sigma,T)\ll T^{15(1-\sigma)/(3+5\sigma)+o(1)}$ for the count of zeros of $\zeta(s)$ with $\Re\rho\ge\sigma$, $|\Im\rho|\le T$. At $\sigma=3/4$ this gives the $T^{30(1-\sigma)/13+o(1)}$ exponent, breaking the long-standing $T^{(12/5)(1-\sigma)+o(1)}$ Huxley-type barrier near the critical line.

cor:1.3.summary: Primes in short intervals: for $y\in[x^{17/30+\epsilon},x^{0.99}]$, $\pi(x+y)-\pi(x)=\frac{y}{\log x}+O_\epsilon(y\exp(-\sqrt[4]{\log x}))$. Improves the Huxley exponent $7/12$ to $17/30$.

cor:1.4.summary: Almost-all short intervals: for $y\in[X^{2/15+\epsilon},X^{0.99}]$, the asymptotic for $\pi(x+y)-\pi(x)$ holds for all but $O(X\exp(-\sqrt[4]{\log x}))$ choices of $x\in[X,2X]$.

conjecture:1.5.summary: Montgomery's large-value conjecture: for $\sigma>1/2$ and $D(t)=\sum_{n=N}^{2N}b_ne^{it\log n}$ with $|b_n|\le1$, a $1$-separated set $W$ with $|D(t)|>N^\sigma$ has $|W|\le C(\sigma)T^{o(1)}N^{2-2\sigma}$. The target; thm:1.1 attains it in a partial range.

thm:1.6.summary: Heath-Brown's mean-value bound: for a $1$-separated $\mathcal{T}$ in an interval of length $T$ and $|a_n|\lessapprox1$, $\sum_{t_1,t_2\in\mathcal{T}}|\sum_{n=N}^{2N}a_n n^{i(t_1-t_2)}|^2\lessapprox|\mathcal{T}|^2N+|\mathcal{T}|N^2+|\mathcal{T}|^{5/4}T^{1/2}N$. The classical large-values input the paper builds on and surpasses.

lem:1.7.summary: Energy formulation: for $N\in[T^{2/3},T]$, $\sigma>1/2$, $D(t)=\sum_{n=N}^{2N}b_n n^{it}$, $|b_n|\le1$, and a $1$-separated $W$ with $|D(t)|>N^\sigma$, the additive energy satisfies $E(W)\le|W|^3N^{1-2\sigma+o(1)}+|W|^2N^{2-2\sigma+o(1)}$. Reduces the large-values problem to bounding $E(W)$.

prop:3.1.summary: Main Proposition: for $\sigma\in[7/10,8/10]$ and a $T^\epsilon$-separated $\mathcal{W}$ in an interval of length $T=N^{6/5}$ with $|\sum_n w(n/N)b_n n^{it}|\ge N^\sigma$, one has $|\mathcal{W}|\le TN^{(12-20\sigma)/5+o_\epsilon(1)}$. The technical statement that the bulk of the paper proves and from which thm:1.1 follows.

lem:4.1.summary: Large values controlled by singular values: with $M_W$ the natural matrix, if $|D_N(t)|\ge N^\sigma$ on $W$ and $|b_n|\le1$ then $|W|\lesssim N^{1-2\sigma}s_1(M_W)^2$. Recasts counting $W$ as bounding the top singular value.

lem:4.2.summary: Singular value via traces: for an $m\times n$ matrix $A$, $s_1(A)\le 2(\mathrm{tr}((AA^*)^3)-\mathrm{tr}(AA^*)^3/m^2)^{1/6}+2(\mathrm{tr}(AA^*)/m)^{1/2}$. The linear-algebra lever turning $s_1(M_W)$ into computable traces.

lem:4.3.summary: Non-stationary phase for $h_t(u)=w(u)^2u^{it}$: $\widehat{h_t}(\xi)\lesssim_j(1+|t|)^j/|\xi|^j$ and $\widehat{h_t}(\xi)\lesssim_j(1+|\xi|)^j/|t|^j$. Decay estimates used throughout to localize the trace sums.

lem:4.4.summary: Hilbert–Schmidt norm: for finite $W$ with $|W|\le N^{O(1)}$, $\mathrm{tr}(M_WM_W^*)=N|W|\|w\|_{L^2}^2+O(N^{-100})$. The quadratic-trace term.

lem:4.5.summary: Cubic trace expansion: for $T^\epsilon$-separated $W$, $\mathrm{tr}((M_WM_W^*)^3)=N^3|W|\|w\|_{L^2}^6+\sum_{m\in\mathbb{Z}^3\setminus\{0\}}I_m+O_\epsilon(T^{-100})$ where $I_m=N^3\sum_{t_1,t_2,t_3\in W}\widehat{h}_{t_1-t_2}(m_1N)\widehat{h}_{t_2-t_3}(m_2N)\widehat{h}_{t_3-t_1}(m_3N)$. Splits the cubic trace into a diagonal main term plus oscillatory $I_m$.

prop:4.6.summary: Reduction to $I_m$: for $T^\epsilon$-separated $W$ with $|D_N(t)|>N^\sigma$, $|W|\lesssim_\epsilon N^{2-2\sigma}+N^{1-2\sigma}(\sum_{m\in\mathbb{Z}^3\setminus\{0\}}I_m)^{1/3}$. Everything reduces to estimating the sum of $I_m$'s.

prop:5.1.summary: $S_1$ bound: the part of the $I_m$ sum where one of the $m_i$ vanishes contributes $S_1=O_\epsilon(T^{-10})$ — negligible.

prop:6.1.summary: $S_2$ bound: the 'one large frequency' part satisfies, for any $k\in\mathbb{N}$, $S_2\lessapprox N^2|W|^2+TN|W|^{2-1/k}+N^2|W|^2(T^{1/2}/|W|^{3/4})^{1/k}$. Handled by an iterated approximate-functional-equation argument.

lem:6.2.summary: Approximate functional equation: for $|t|\sim T_0\ge T^\epsilon$, $|\sum_{m\ne0}\widehat{h}_t(mN)|\lesssim T_0^{-1/2}\int_{u\lessapprox1}|\sum_{m\lessapprox T_0/N}m^{-i(t+u)}|du+O(T^{-100})$. Trades a long sum for a shorter dual one — the engine of the $S_2$ iteration.

prop:7.1.summary: Cancellation within $I_m$: $|I_m|\lesssim N^3\int_{|m_1v_1+m_2v_2+m_3|\lessapprox1/N,\,v_1\asymp v_2\asymp1}|R(v_1)R(v_2/v_1)R(v_2)|dv_1dv_2+O(T^{-200})$, and if $|m_1|\le|m_2|\le|m_3|$ then $|I_m|=O(T^{-200})$ unless $|m_2|\asymp|m_3|$. The 'key cancellation' that makes $S_3$ tractable.

prop:7.2.summary: Expansion of $S_3$: there is $0<M_1\le M\lessapprox T/N$ with $S_3\lessapprox\frac{N^2}{M}\sum_{|m_1|\sim M_1,\,|m_2|,|m_3|\sim M}\widetilde I_m+O(T^{-100})$ for an explicit $\widetilde I_m$ built from $R$ and $\widetilde R_M$. Reorganizes the 'all-frequencies-large' contribution into a dyadic sum amenable to $L^2$/$L^4$ control.

prop:8.1.summary: $S_3$ controlled by energy: for $T^\epsilon$-separated $W$ in an interval of length $T$, $S_3\lessapprox T^2|W|^{1/2}E(W)^{1/2}$. Bridges the trace machinery to the additive energy $E(W)$.

lem:8.2.summary: $L^2$ bound: $\int_{v\asymp1}|R(v)|^2dv\lesssim_\epsilon|W|$ for $T^\epsilon$-separated $W$. Plancherel-type input for prop:8.1.

lem:8.3.summary: $L^4$ bound: $\int_{v\asymp1}|\widetilde R_M(v)|^4dv\lessapprox E(W)$ and $\int_{v\asymp1}|R(v)|^4dv\lessapprox E(W)$. The fourth-moment input that introduces $E(W)$ into the $S_3$ estimate.

prop:9.1.summary: Equidistribution over affine transformations: if $f\ge0$ is supported on $u\asymp1$ with $|\widehat f(\xi)|\lessapprox_j(|\xi|/T)^j$, then $J(f)\lessapprox M^6(\int f)^2+M^4\int f^2$. The summation-over-$\mathrm{GL}_2$-type lemma that powers the refined $S_3$ bound.

lem:9.2.summary: Iterative bound for $J(f)$: $J(f)\lessapprox M^6(\int f)^2+(M^4\int f^2)^{1/2}J(\widetilde f)^{1/2}$ with $\widetilde f$ a smoothed version of $f$. Bootstrapped to prove prop:9.1.

prop:10.1.summary: Refined $S_3$ bound: $S_3\lessapprox T^2|W|^{3/2}+TN|W|^{1/2}E(W)^{1/2}$. Sharpens prop:8.1 using the affine-equidistribution lemma.

prop:11.1.summary: Energy bound: for $D_N(t)=\sum_{n\sim N}b_n n^{it}$, $|b_n|\le1$, a $1$-separated $W$ in an interval of length $T$ with $|D_N(t)|\ge N^\sigma$, and $T^{3/4}\le N\le T$, $E(W)\lessapprox|W|N^{4-4\sigma}+|W|^{21/8}T^{1/4}N^{1-2\sigma}+|W|^3N^{1-2\sigma}$. The crucial new additive-energy estimate.

prop:11.2.summary: Final $S_3$ bound for $N\ge T^{3/4}$: $S_3\lessapprox T^2|W|^{3/2}+T|W|N^{3-2\sigma}+T|W|^2N^{3/2-\sigma}+T^{9/8}|W|^{29/16}N^{3/2-\sigma}$. Combines prop:10.1 with prop:11.1.

lem:11.3.summary: Dirichlet polynomials don't vary fast: $|D_N(t)|\lessapprox\int_{|u-t|\lessapprox1}|D_N(u)|du+O(T^{-100})$. Lets discrete sets be replaced by averages.

lem:11.4.summary: Energy via discrete third moment: $E(W)\lessapprox N^{-2\sigma}\sum_{n_1,n_2\sim N}|R(n_1/n_2)|^3$. Converts $E(W)$ into a divisor-type sum split by $\gcd(n_1,n_2)$.

lem:11.6.summary: Discrete fourth moment: $\sum_{n_1,n_2\sim M}|R(n_1/n_2)|^4\lessapprox M^2E(W)+|W|^4M+E(W)^{3/4}|W|T^{1/2}M$. Self-referential in $E(W)$ — closed by the bootstrap.

lem:11.7.summary: Smoothing for $R$: for $v\asymp1$, $|R(v)|\lesssim T\int_{|v'-v|\lessapprox1/T}|R(v')|dv'+O(T^{-100})$. Discrete-to-continuous transfer for the divisor sums.

lem:11.8.summary: Small-GCD terms: $\sum_{n_1,n_2\sim N,\,\gcd(n_1,n_2)\le D}|R(n_1/n_2)|^3\lessapprox(DT+N^2)|W|^{1/2}E(W)^{1/2}$. The 'generic' part of the third-moment sum.

lem:11.9.summary: Large-GCD terms: for $D=N^2/T$ and $N\ge T^{3/4}$, $\sum_{n_1,n_2\sim N,\,\gcd(n_1,n_2)\ge D}|R(n_1/n_2)|^3\lesssim N|W|^3+NT^{1/4}|W|^{21/8}+E(W)^{1/2}|W|^{1/2}N^2$. The structured part; together with lem:11.8 it yields the energy bound prop:11.1.

prop:12.1.summary: Large values for $N\ge T^{5/6}$: under the hypotheses of thm:1.1 with $T^{5/6}\le N\le T$, $V=N^\sigma$, $\sigma\ge7/10$, $R\lessapprox N^{2-2\sigma}+T^{1/2}N^{3-4\sigma}+\inf_k(\dots)$, in particular $R\lessapprox N^{2-2\sigma}+T^{1/2}N^{3-4\sigma}+T^{(30\sigma-21)/5}N^{(46-60\sigma)/5}$. Combines prop:3.1 with the $S_2$ optimization to give thm:1.1 in the hard range.

edges.note: The large-values estimate in the critical range $N\ge T^{5/6}$ is exactly prop:12.1; smaller $N$ reduce to Heath-Brown.
For $N$ away from the critical window the bound is supplied by Heath-Brown's classical mean-value estimate.
The zero-density estimate is deduced from the new large-values bound via the standard zero-detection / mean-value argument.
Primes in short intervals follow from the zero-density estimate by the explicit-formula / Perron argument.
Almost-all short intervals follow from the same zero-density input with a second-moment averaging.
thm:1.1 establishes Montgomery's conjecture in a partial range of $(\sigma,N,T)$.
prop:12.1 is the $\sigma\in[7/10,8/10]$, $T=N^{6/5}$ Main Proposition combined with an $S_2$-parameter optimization.
The $\inf_k$ term comes from optimizing the $S_2$ bound.
The energy formulation of the problem is exactly the energy bound prop:11.1 phrased for the reader's orientation.
The Main Proposition is proved by feeding the trace-reduction $|W|\lesssim N^{2-2\sigma}+N^{1-2\sigma}(\sum I_m)^{1/3}$ the $S_1+S_2+S_3$ bounds.
Contribution of $S_1$ is negligible.
Contribution of $S_2$.
Contribution of $S_3$ (final form for $N\ge T^{3/4}$).
Counting $W$ is reduced to bounding the top singular value of $M_W$.
The singular value is bounded by cubic and quadratic traces.
Quadratic trace evaluated by the Hilbert–Schmidt computation.
Cubic trace expanded into the diagonal main term plus $\sum_m I_m$.
Non-stationary phase decay isolates the relevant frequencies in the trace expansion.
Same decay estimates control the error term.
Vanishing of $S_1$ is pure non-stationary-phase decay.
The $S_2$ bound is obtained by iterating the approximate functional equation.
Phase decay underlies the dual-sum trade.
Final $S_3$ bound starts from the affine-equidistribution-refined estimate.
...and substitutes the new energy bound.
Sharpens the energy-controlled $S_3$ bound.
The improvement uses equidistribution of affine transformations.
prop:9.1 is proved by iterating lem:9.2.
$S_3$ is first written as the dyadic $\widetilde I_m$ sum...
...then bounded via the $L^4$ estimate, which introduces $E(W)$.
$L^2$ estimate handles the remaining factor.
The expansion exploits the key cancellation in each $I_m$.
Starts from the cubic-trace expansion's $I_m$ definition.
Cancellation is a stationary/non-stationary phase analysis of $I_m$.
Energy is reduced to the discrete third moment of $R(n_1/n_2)$.
Small-GCD part of the third moment.
Large-GCD part of the third moment.
Hölder + the discrete fourth moment bounds the generic terms.
Fourth-moment $L^4$ control reused.
Large-GCD terms reduce to a shorter sum controlled by the discrete second moment.
The discrete second moment is Heath-Brown's bound applied at scale $M$.
Discrete-to-continuous transfer before applying $L^4$.
Replaces the discrete set $W$ by an average of $|D_N|$.
Same smoothing step.

main_results.description: The large-values estimate and its number-theoretic payoffs: zero density and primes in short intervals.

trace_method.description: Recast counting the large-value set as bounding the top singular value of $M_W$; expand via traces into a diagonal main term plus the oscillatory sum $\sum_m I_m$.

S1_S2.description: The easy ($S_1$, negligible) and the 'one large frequency' ($S_2$) parts of the $I_m$ sum, the latter via an iterated approximate functional equation.

S3_cancellation_and_energy.description: The hard 'all frequencies large' part $S_3$: a key cancellation in each $I_m$, a dyadic expansion, reduction to the additive energy $E(W)$ via $L^2$/$L^4$ estimates, and an affine-equidistribution refinement.

energy_bound.description: The new additive-energy estimate: reduce $E(W)$ to a discrete third moment of $R(n_1/n_2)$ and split by $\gcd(n_1,n_2)$ into small- and large-GCD contributions.

assembly.description: Optimize the $S_2$ parameter and assemble the pieces into the critical-range bound.

HB.title: A large values estimate for Dirichlet polynomials

Hux.title: On the difference between consecutive primes

Mont.title: Topics in Multiplicative Number Theory (large value conjecture)

Ivic.title: The Riemann Zeta-Function: Theory and Applications

BDG.title: Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three
